meta.game: Los Angeles Lakers at Golden State Warriors

LAL.headline: Redick: 'Nothing personal' about heated exchange with Vanderbilt
LAL.description: JJ Redick talks about the exchanging of words he had with Jarred Vanderbilt during the Lakers' loss to the Thunder.

LAL.headline: JJ Redick on Deandre Ayton: 'He's had trouble catching the ball'
LAL.description: JJ Redick bluntly addresses Deandre Ayton's struggles after the Lakers' 123-87 loss to the Thunder.

LAL.headline: NBA Power Rankings: Did your team meet or miss its season expectations?
LAL.description: The last NBA power rankings of the regular season! Let's see where all 30 teams stack up before the postseason.

LAL.headline: Lakers' Redick downplays quarrel with Vanderbilt as 'normal'
LAL.description: Lakers coach JJ Redick subbed forward Jarred Vanderbilt out of early in the second quarter, leading to a heated exchange between the two that Redick described as a "normal interaction" for him.

LAL.headline: Ayton, Lakers square off against the Warriors
LAL.description: Los Angeles Lakers (50-28, fourth in the Western Conference) vs. Golden State Warriors (37-42, 10th in the Western Conference)

GS.headline: NBA Power Rankings: Did your team meet or miss its season expectations?
GS.description: The last NBA power rankings of the regular season! Let's see where all 30 teams stack up before the postseason.

GS.headline: What NBA players, coaches and execs are saying about tanking
GS.description: With tanking strategies growing in both audaciousness and frequency of use, can the league get its arms around the often-effective practice?

GS.headline: Ayton, Lakers square off against the Warriors
GS.description: Los Angeles Lakers (50-28, fourth in the Western Conference) vs. Golden State Warriors (37-42, 10th in the Western Conference)

GS.headline: Sacramento Kings vs. Golden State Warriors: Game Highlights
GS.description: Sacramento Kings vs. Golden State Warriors: Game Highlights

GS.headline: Curry scores 17 points with two 4-point plays in second game back from injury, Warriors beat Kings
GS.description: — Stephen Curry scored 17 points with a pair of four-point plays in his second game back from a right knee injury, and the Golden State Warriors held off the Sacramento Kings 110-105 on Tuesday night to snap a four-game losing streak.
